publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Exception handling and propagation; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Exception handling and propagation: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? A bare except around a whole function is robust because it prevents all crashes and lets execution continue.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Catch specific exceptions at a responsible boundary, keep the try block narrow, preserve causal context, and recover only when a valid next state exists.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Catch specific exceptions at a responsible boundary, keep the try block narrow, preserve causal context, and recover only when a valid next state exists.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Catch specific exceptions at a responsible boundary, keep the try block narrow, preserve causal context, and recover only when a valid next state exists.
A bare except around a whole function is robust because it prevents all crashes and lets execution continue.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'keep the try block narrow'. Catch specific exceptions at a responsible boundary, keep the try block narrow, preserve causal context, and recover only when a valid next state exists.
